title: Security Level Gap Assessment Framework
verificationNote: SL gap assessment framework constructed from IEC 62443-3-3 foundational requirements and system requirements. Security level descriptions are paraphrased. Obtain normative text from IEC. Assessment criteria are indicative guidance, not certification requirements.
description: Interactive tool for assessing the gap between current Security Level (SL-A, achieved) and target Security Level (SL-T) per IEC 62443-3-3. The assessment covers all 7 Foundational Requirements (FR1-FR7) and provides specific criteria for each SL 1-4 level.

assessmentMethodology.approach: For each Foundational Requirement, assess the current achieved Security Level (SL-A) against the target Security Level (SL-T). The gap between SL-A and SL-T identifies required security improvements. SL-T is determined by IEC 62443-3-2 risk assessment (zone-based).

slDefinitions.SL 0: No specific requirements or security protection necessary
slDefinitions.SL 1: Protection against casual or coincidental violation
slDefinitions.SL 2: Protection against intentional violation using simple means with low resources, generic skills, and low motivation
slDefinitions.SL 3: Protection against intentional violation using sophisticated means with moderate resources, IACS-specific skills, and moderate motivation
slDefinitions.SL 4: Protection against intentional violation using sophisticated means with extended resources, IACS-specific skills, and high motivation

steps: 1. Define zones and conduits per IEC 62443-3-2
2. Determine SL-T (target) for each zone based on risk assessment
3. Assess SL-A (achieved) for each zone against each FR using criteria below
5. Define remediation plan to close gaps with prioritisation
6. Reassess periodically (minimum annually per NACSA Act 854 s23)

foundationalRequirements.name: Identification and Authentication Control
foundationalRequirements.description: Identify and authenticate all users (humans, software processes, and devices) before allowing access to the IACS.

SL1: Unique user identification for all human users
Password-based authentication for interactive access
Default credentials changed on all devices
Account lockout after repeated failed attempts
SL2: All SL 1 requirements plus:
Multi-factor authentication for remote and privileged access
Unique identification for software processes and devices
Centralised authentication where architecturally feasible
Password complexity and rotation policies enforced
Authenticator management (secure storage, revocation)
SL3: All SL 2 requirements plus:
Multi-factor authentication for all interactive access (not just remote)
Hardware-based authenticators for critical system access
Certificate-based device authentication
Federated identity management across OT zones
Continuous authentication / session revalidation for critical operations
SL4: All SL 3 requirements plus:
Multi-factor authentication with at least one physical factor for all access
Biometric or hardware token authentication for safety system access
Mutual authentication between all communicating entities
Cryptographic device identity with hardware root of trust
Real-time authentication anomaly detection

assessmentCriteria.criterion: Are all human users uniquely identified?
Are default credentials eliminated on all devices?
Is MFA enforced for remote access?
Are software processes and devices uniquely identified?
Is MFA enforced for ALL interactive access?
Is certificate-based device authentication deployed?
Is mutual authentication used between all entities?
Is hardware root of trust used for device identity?

foundationalRequirements.name: Use Control
foundationalRequirements.description: Enforce assigned privileges of an authenticated user (human, software process, or device) to perform the requested action on the IACS and monitor the use of these privileges.

SL1: Authorisation enforcement for all user access
Role-based access control (RBAC) defined
Least privilege principle applied
Wireless access restricted and controlled
SL2: All SL 1 requirements plus:
Granular role definitions (operator, engineer, supervisor, administrator)
Portable and mobile device restrictions
Session lock after inactivity timeout
Auditing of privilege use for security-relevant actions
Explicit authorisation for remote access sessions
SL3: All SL 2 requirements plus:
Dual authorisation for critical operations (e.g., SIS modification)
Supervisor approval required for safety-relevant changes
Time-limited privilege elevation
Comprehensive audit trail of all privilege use
Access control decisions logged and analysable
SL4: All SL 3 requirements plus:
Multi-person authorisation for highest-criticality operations
Real-time monitoring and alerting on privilege escalation
Automated enforcement of separation of duties
Privilege use analytics and anomaly detection
Hardware-enforced access control where feasible

assessmentCriteria.criterion: Is RBAC defined and enforced?
Is least privilege applied?
Are session lock timeouts configured?
Are privilege-use actions audited?
Is dual authorisation required for safety changes?
Is there comprehensive audit trail of all privilege use?
Is multi-person authorisation enforced for critical operations?
Is automated separation of duties enforced?

foundationalRequirements.name: System Integrity
foundationalRequirements.description: Ensure the integrity of the IACS to prevent unauthorised manipulation.

SL1: Communication integrity for critical control messages
Malware protection on Windows/Linux-based OT systems
Input validation on all IACS interfaces
Deterministic output on error conditions (fail-safe)
SL2: All SL 1 requirements plus:
Cryptographic integrity verification for software and firmware updates
Application whitelisting on HMI and engineering workstations
Session integrity protection
OT-vendor-approved antimalware with OT-safe configuration
Integrity monitoring for critical system files and configurations
SL3: All SL 2 requirements plus:
Cryptographic communication integrity for all control protocols where supported
Automated integrity verification of controller programs
Code signing for all deployed software
Hardware-based integrity measurement (TPM where available)
Real-time file integrity monitoring with alerting
SL4: All SL 3 requirements plus:
End-to-end cryptographic integrity for all communications
Hardware root of trust for boot integrity (secure boot)
Continuous automated integrity verification of all IACS components
Tamper-evident hardware for critical controllers
Supply chain integrity verification with cryptographic provenance

assessmentCriteria.criterion: Is malware protection deployed on OT workstations?
Is input validation applied on IACS interfaces?
Is application whitelisting deployed?
Are software updates cryptographically verified?
Is automated controller program integrity verification deployed?
Is code signing enforced for all software?
Is secure boot / hardware root of trust used?
Is continuous automated integrity verification operational?

foundationalRequirements.name: Data Confidentiality
foundationalRequirements.description: Ensure the confidentiality of information on communication channels and in data repositories to prevent unauthorised disclosure.

SL1: Information confidentiality awareness (classification policy)
Identification of confidential OT data (process recipes, configurations, IP)
SL2: All SL 1 requirements plus:
Encryption of data at rest for sensitive OT data (recipes, configurations)
Encryption of data in transit for remote access and IDMZ traversal
Secure disposal of OT storage media
SL3: All SL 2 requirements plus:
Encryption of data in transit for all cross-zone communications
Key management procedures established
Data loss prevention controls for OT intellectual property
Encrypted backups of OT configurations and programs
SL4: All SL 3 requirements plus:
End-to-end encryption for all OT communications where protocol supports it
Hardware security modules (HSM) for key storage
Cryptographic protection against data exfiltration
Residual information protection (secure memory clearing)

assessmentCriteria.criterion: Is confidential OT data identified and classified?
Is remote access traffic encrypted?
Is sensitive data at rest encrypted?
Are all cross-zone communications encrypted?
Is key management formally established?
Are HSMs used for key storage?
Is end-to-end encryption deployed for all OT communications?

foundationalRequirements.name: Restricted Data Flow
foundationalRequirements.description: Segment the IACS environment via zones and conduits to limit unnecessary data flow.

SL1: Network segmentation between OT and IT (at minimum a firewall)
Documented network architecture diagram
Basic firewall rules between OT and IT
SL2: All SL 1 requirements plus:
Zone and conduit model defined per IEC 62443-3-2
IDMZ implemented between OT and IT
Deny-by-default firewall rules at zone boundaries
No direct IT-to-OT network paths
Documented and approved data flows for each conduit
SL3: All SL 2 requirements plus:
Data diode for OT-to-IT data export (critical zones)
Deep packet inspection (DPI) at zone boundaries for OT protocols
Microsegmentation within OT (separate VLANs per functional area)
Network traffic baselining and anomaly detection
Regular review of all zone boundary firewall rules
SL4: All SL 3 requirements plus:
Hardware-enforced unidirectional gateways for all OT-to-IT data flows
OT-aware intrusion prevention at all zone boundaries
Automated network topology verification against documented architecture
Zero-trust network architecture principles within OT
Real-time detection of any unauthorised cross-zone traffic

assessmentCriteria.criterion: Is OT network segmented from IT?
Is there a documented network architecture?
Is an IDMZ implemented?
Are deny-by-default firewall rules at zone boundaries?
Are data diodes used for critical OT-to-IT flows?
Is deep packet inspection deployed for OT protocols?
Are hardware-enforced unidirectional gateways deployed?
Is automated network topology verification operational?

foundationalRequirements.name: Timely Response to Events
foundationalRequirements.description: Respond to security violations by notifying the proper authority, reporting needed evidence of the violation, and taking timely corrective action.

SL1: Audit log capability for security-relevant events
Logs protected from unauthorised modification
Basic log review process established
SL2: All SL 1 requirements plus:
Continuous audit logging of security events across all IACS components
Centralised log collection (syslog or SIEM)
Alerting on critical security events
Log retention policy defined (minimum per NACSA requirements)
Incident response procedure for OT security events
SL3: All SL 2 requirements plus:
OT-specific SIEM with ICS/OT correlation rules
Automated alerting with escalation procedures
Forensic-grade audit logging (timestamps, event correlation)
Regular log analysis and threat hunting in OT environment
Integration with NACSA s26 notification procedure (6-hour initial notification)
SL4: All SL 3 requirements plus:
Real-time security event correlation across all IACS zones
Automated incident response for predefined attack patterns
Continuous monitoring with 24/7 OT SOC capability
Machine learning anomaly detection on OT network traffic
Evidence preservation automation for cyber-physical incidents

assessmentCriteria.criterion: Is audit logging enabled for security events?
Are logs protected from modification?
Is centralised log collection (SIEM) deployed?
Is an OT incident response procedure defined?
Is OT-specific SIEM with ICS rules deployed?
Is NACSA s26 notification procedure integrated?
Is 24/7 OT SOC capability operational?
Is automated incident response deployed?

foundationalRequirements.name: Resource Availability
foundationalRequirements.description: Ensure the availability of the IACS against the degradation or denial of essential services.

SL1: Denial of Service (DoS) protection at network boundaries
Backup and recovery procedures for critical IACS components
IACS component inventory maintained
Defined emergency procedures for OT system failure
SL2: All SL 1 requirements plus:
Network-level DoS protection (rate limiting, traffic filtering)
Redundancy for critical control systems (controller failover)
Tested backup and recovery with defined RTO/RPO
Comprehensive IACS asset inventory with configuration baselines
OT-specific disaster recovery plan
Patch and vulnerability management programme
SL3: All SL 2 requirements plus:
DDoS mitigation at network perimeter
High availability for all safety-critical control systems
Automated failover for critical SCADA/DCS
Regular disaster recovery testing (minimum annually)
Proactive vulnerability assessment and remediation
Spare parts and configuration backup for rapid recovery
SL4: All SL 3 requirements plus:
Diversity in control system redundancy (different vendors/platforms)
Automated attack mitigation and service recovery
Continuous availability monitoring with predictive failure detection
Zero-downtime patching capability where feasible
Cyber-resilience testing including destructive testing

assessmentCriteria.criterion: Is DoS protection at network boundaries?
Are backup/recovery procedures defined and tested?
Is a comprehensive asset inventory maintained?
Is redundancy provided for critical controllers?
Is high availability for safety-critical systems?
Is DR testing performed at least annually?
Is vendor diversity in control system redundancy?
Is continuous availability monitoring with predictive failure?

gapAssessmentTemplate.description: Template for conducting SL gap assessment per zone. For each zone, assess current SL-A against target SL-T for each Foundational Requirement.

priorityScale.Critical: SL-A = 0 and SL-T >= 2, or safety-related gap
priorityScale.Low: SL-A meets SL-T but improvement opportunities exist
